docs.title: Yozara Financial Tracking Dashboard
docs.text: Welcome to Yozara, your all-in-one financial tracking dashboard designed to streamline your financial data, track spending, and optimize budgeting. Yozara empowers you to make data-driven decisions with real-time analytics and customizable dashboards.

docs.title: 📌 Get Started
docs.text:
• Learn how to set up Yozara in the Usage Guide.
  • View detailed updates in the Release Notes.

docs.title: 🚀 Core Features
docs.text:
• Real-Time Financial Tracking: Monitor expenses, income, and budgets in real time.
  • Custom Dashboards: Create and organize dashboards with widgets that matter to you.
  • Advanced Analytics: Visualize your spending habits, identify trends, and predict future costs.
  • Multi-Currency Support: Seamlessly switch between currencies for global financial insights.
  • Collaborative Sharing: Share financial dashboards with team members or clients securely.

docs.title: 💡 Need Help?
docs.text: Visit the Usage Guide to learn more about configuring Yozara to fit your financial tracking needs.

docs.title: Designing AI-Ready Developer Documentation
docs.text: A deep dive guide to building structured, searchable, and composable documentation systems.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Modern documentation must serve multiple consumers simultaneously:

  • Developers reading in a browser
  • In-product help systems embedding contextual guidance
  • AI systems retrieving structured answers from documentation corpora

If documentation is not intentionally structured, retrieval systems produce incomplete answers, hallucinations, and inconsistent responses.

AI-ready documentation is about designing documentation systems that are:

  • Structurally predictable
  • Context-rich
  • Chunkable
  • Version-aware
  • Audience-aware

docs.title: Intended Audience
docs.text:
This guide is designed for:

  • Developer Experience teams
  • Technical writers
  • Platform engineers
  • Documentation architects

It assumes familiarity with static site generators such as Docusaurus or MkDocs.

docs.title: Task Completion Speed
docs.text:
Documentation should optimize for implementation velocity. Developers need:

  • Clear prerequisites
  • Runnable examples
  • Minimal context switching
  • Fast scanning

Every guide should enable a developer to move from reading to working code efficiently.

docs.title: Predictable Structure
docs.text:
Every documentation page should answer the following questions:

  • What is this?
  • When should I use it?
  • How do I implement it?
  • What are the edge cases?
  • What breaks if I misuse it?

Predictability reduces cognitive load and improves search effectiveness.

docs.title: Progressive Disclosure
docs.text:
Separate content types clearly:

  • Overview
  • Concept
  • Implementation
  • Reference
  • Troubleshooting

Avoid mixing conceptual explanation and API reference without clear structural boundaries.

docs.title: Documentation Architecture
docs.text: A scalable documentation system should follow a predictable taxonomy.

docs.title: Recommended Structure
docs.text:
Documentation
│
├── Getting Started
├── Concepts
├── Guides
├── API Reference
├── Release Notes
└── Troubleshooting

Clear taxonomy improves:

  • Navigation consistency
  • Search precision
  • AI chunk alignment
  • Version traceability

docs.title: Writing for Retrieval Systems
docs.text: AI systems typically retrieve content in segmented chunks. Poor structure reduces retrieval precision.

docs.title: Use Consistent Section Headings
docs.text:
Every feature guide should follow a consistent pattern:

# Feature Name
## Overview
## Prerequisites
## Implementation
## Example
## Error Handling
## Limitations

Headings serve as semantic anchors for both human readers and AI systems.

docs.title: Avoid Ambiguous Language
docs.text:
Ambiguity reduces retrieval quality.

Instead of:

This setting controls the timeout.

Write:

The requestTimeout parameter controls how long the API waits before terminating an outbound HTTP request. Default: 30 seconds.

Always specify:

  • Parameter name
  • Scope
  • Default value
  • Units

docs.title: Versioning Strategy
docs.text: Documentation must reflect API and SDK version stability.

docs.title: Use Immutable Version Paths
docs.text:
/docs/v1/
/docs/v2/
/docs/latest/

Best practices:

  • Never overwrite documentation for a live API version
  • Freeze documentation at release
  • Maintain backward-compatible access

docs.title: Deep Dive Example: Webhook Retry Policy
Overview
docs.text:
The webhook retry policy determines how failed webhook events are re-delivered.

Retries occur when:

  • The endpoint returns HTTP 5xx
  • The endpoint times out
  • DNS resolution fails

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
PATCH /v2/webhooks/{id}

{
  "retryPolicy": {
    "maxAttempts": 7,
    "initialDelaySeconds": 10
  }
}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Return HTTP 2xx quickly
  • Process events asynchronously
  • Log webhook IDs for traceability

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
Developer experience is a systems design problem.

Well-structured documentation:

  • Reduces support burden
  • Accelerates integrations
  • Improves search relevance
  • Enables reliable AI assistance

Documentation is not static content. It is a product surface that must be intentionally designed.

docs.title: Yozara Usage Guide
docs.text: This guide will walk you through the setup and configuration of Yozara, helping you unlock its full potential.

docs.title: 📌 1️⃣ Setting Up Yozara
docs.text:
1. Sign in to your Yozara account.
  2. Navigate to the Dashboard.
  3. Click Add Widget to start customizing your view.
  4. Select from options such as:
  5. Expense Tracker
  6. Income Overview
  7. Budget Planner
  8. Savings Goals

docs.title: 📌 2️⃣ Customizing Your Dashboard
docs.text:
• Drag and drop widgets to organize your view.
  • Resize and adjust widgets to prioritize key data.
  • Apply filters to narrow down data views.

docs.title: 📌 3️⃣ Generating Financial Reports
docs.text:
• Click Reports in the main navigation.
  • Choose a Date Range and Report Type.
  • Export reports as PDF, CSV, or Excel.

docs.title: 📌 4️⃣ Multi-Currency Support
docs.text:
• Navigate to Settings → Currency.
  • Choose your preferred currency for real-time conversion.

docs.title: 📌 5️⃣ Sharing Dashboards
docs.text:
• Click the Share icon on your dashboard.
  • Enter the recipient's email address.
  • Choose permission levels: View Only or Edit Access.

docs.title: 🔄 Troubleshooting & Support
docs.text: If you encounter issues or have questions, reach out to Yozara Support or visit our documentation for troubleshooting steps.
